display.purpose: Hero headline only. 'Never Miss a Goal.' One instance per page.

title.purpose: Component labels in Goal Green — pillar titles, live state heading.

body.purpose: Primary prose. Max 65–75ch line length enforced on containers.

label.purpose: Countdown labels, unit text, badge text. Always uppercase. Highest letter-spacing in the system.

shadows.name: goal-glow
shadows.purpose: Ambient glow on countdown blocks via ::after pseudo. Signals 'alive', not elevation. Not interactive.

shadows.name: hero-ambient
shadows.purpose: Background bloom behind hero content. Creates center focus without card structure. Not a shadow.

motion.name: dot-pulse
motion.purpose: Live Now badge indicator dot. The only animation in the system when live state is active. Requires reduced-motion fallback.

motion.name: cta-hover
motion.purpose: CTA button hover: opacity 0.88, translateY(-1px). No shadow on hover.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Nav and hero padding reduction, countdown block minimum-width scaling.

breakpoints.name: pillars-3col
breakpoints.purpose: Pillars grid switches from 1 to 3 columns.

components.name: CTA Button
components.description: Primary action — Open App. Goal Green fill, black text, uppercase Inter 600. The most important element on the page.
components.html: Open App
components.css: .ds-cta-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; background: #00e05a; color: #000; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; padding: 16px 40px; border-radius: 4px; transition: opacity 0.2s, transform 0.2s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-cta-btn:hover { opacity: 0.88; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-cta-btn:active { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } .ds-cta-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00e05a; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Countdown Block
components.description: Single countdown unit — number + label. Dugout Grey background, Bebas Neue number, Inter label.
components.css: .ds-cd-block { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-cd-number { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 72px; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #fff; background: #111111; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 6px; padding: 14px 18px; min-width: 72px; text-align: center; position: relative; } .ds-cd-number::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; border-radius: 6px; box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(0,224,90,0.08); } .ds-cd-unit { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #fff; }

components.name: Live Badge
components.description: Shown when the World Cup is underway. Pill badge with pulsing green dot — the only animation in the live state.
components.css: .ds-live-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: rgba(0,224,90,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(0,224,90,0.25); border-radius: 100px; padding: 8px 20px; } .ds-live-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: #00e05a; animation: ds-pulse 1.6s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%,100%{opacity:1;transform:scale(1)} 50%{opacity:0.4;transform:scale(0.8)} } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-live-dot { animation: none; } } .ds-live-badge-text { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #00e05a; }

components.name: Pillar Feature Cell
components.description: One cell of the three-pillar features grid. Match Dark background, green dot accent, Bebas Neue title in Goal Green.
components.html:
Goal Chance

Tracks live match intensity and tells you when a goal is unlikely.
components.css: .ds-pillar { background: #080808; padding: 36px 28px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; text-align: center; gap: 10px; } .ds-pillar-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #00e05a; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-pillar-title { font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 20px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #00e05a; margin: 0; } .ds-pillar-body { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 300; color: #fff; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Brand Logo
components.description: The NeverMiss wordmark. 'Never' in Goal Green, 'Miss' in Ink White. The brand split is the logo.
components.css: .ds-brand-logo { font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 28px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-decoration: none; } .ds-brand-prefix { color: #00e05a; } .ds-brand-suffix { color: #fff; }

narrative.overview:
NeverMiss is built for the fan who's half-watching from the couch and half-managing real life. The visual system behaves like a mate who's already thought of everything: no fuss, no fanfare, just a quick heads-up and you're sorted. Every design decision reflects that relationship — direct, confident, self-aware enough to have a sense of humor about bathroom breaks.

The palette is stadium-night dark, not because dark is aesthetically interesting, but because fans watch in dim rooms with phones at 40% brightness. The single accent — a vivid, alert-signal green — earns its impact by appearing sparingly. Typography commits fully to the display-body split: Bebas Neue carries event energy; Inter handles the human part.

This system explicitly rejects three aesthetic families the user named: the over-produced corporate sports brand (clean surface, zero soul); the SaaS landing-page pattern (hero metrics, gradient text, '10× your match experience'); and hypebeast streetwear maximalism (all-caps stacking, aggressive decoration, nothing left to breathe).
keyCharacteristics: Near-black body, one sharp green accent, white ink — three ingredients, no filler
Bebas Neue at display scale: event-energy type that reads at arm's length
Inter Light for body: readable, low fatigue, no-fuss
Sparse decoration: radial green glow on the hero, 1px borders on blocks, nothing else
Direct copy: irreverent, specific, clearly fan-authored

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Goal Green is the go-signal. It appears only where the interface means 'safe' or 'action': the CTA, the live indicator, brand type. Using it decoratively is forbidden. Rarity earns meaning.

rules.name: The Two-Layer Rule
rules.body: Exactly two surface depths: Match Dark (body) and Dugout Grey (inset). No new surfaces, no tonal expansion. There is no third layer.

rules.name: The Voice-Split Rule
rules.body: Bebas Neue speaks for the event; Inter speaks for the human. Bebas Neue never runs at body scale. Inter never runs at display scale. The voices stay categorically distinct.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. The green glow is ambient, not reactive. No shadow lifts on hover — the CTA button hovers via opacity and translateY only. Structural shadows are prohibited.

dos: Do run body text at full Ink White (#ffffff) on Match Dark. 21:1 contrast; no qualification needed.
Do keep Goal Green on ≤15% of any surface. Its rarity is the brand.
Do use Bebas Neue for event-scale headings only (≥18px). Inter for everything else.
Do apply the Two-Layer Rule: Match Dark below, Dugout Grey for insets, nothing else.
Do use the gap-as-border pattern for the pillars grid. No individual card borders or backgrounds.
Do write copy that's specific and self-aware. 'Dropping a deuce' is on-brand. 'Step away at a safe moment' is not.
Do add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) for all animations.
donts: Don't reproduce FIFA official / corporate aesthetics: sponsor-blue palettes, trophy renders, 'The Beautiful Game' language, dense stats UI.
Don't use SaaS landing-page patterns: hero metric templates, background-clip gradient text, numbered section eyebrows (01/02/03), cream or parchment backgrounds.
Don't use hypebeast streetwear patterns: all-caps stacking on every element, aggressive drop-shadow accumulation, border-left stripe accents.
Don't add a third surface depth. No new tinted containers, no modal-grey.
Don't use Goal Green for decoration — icon fills, arbitrary highlights, gradient endpoints.
Don't use glassmorphism or backdrop-filter decoratively.
Don't pair Inter with itself at different weights to create a heading — that's Bebas Neue's job.
